steps.title: Spot the unifying purpose
steps.content:
The passage contrasts Villasana's two subject types—famous people like Frida Kahlo and ordinary people like a woman with a baby.

But the final sentence reveals their shared meaning: she sees “both of these approaches as ways of depicting power and interconnectedness of all people.” The main idea is the common purpose both subjects serve.

steps.title: Match the unified contrast
steps.content:
Choice A directly states Villasana focuses on “both famous and everyday examples of human strength and connection”—matching the two subject types and their unified purpose (strength = power; connection = interconnectedness).

The other choices invent a career progression (B), inappropriately narrow to one work (C), or reference information not in the passage (D).

That matches choice A — A is correct.
